There are multiple agents running, and the scroll on the left-hand side for the agents is not working. I'm not able to see what agents are running at any point in time.

Ideally, when an agent that has spawned off has successfully completed, the orchestrator agent should ideally get a signal, and it should terminate that particular agent so that you are able to manage the life cycle of the agents properly. I don't think that is happening right now, and the user has to prompt every time when an agent is completed for you to take action again.

I'm thinking that as we move towards the execution phase, I want to provide a highly differentiated experience where we are able to see the activities and the features getting completed and transitioning from getting picked up to getting processed, etc., and also organizing the details within phases. Things are happening in parallel, and things are getting picked up by multiple agents. I want to see that basically flowing in a Kanban kind of a style or in some other style, which will give the user the perspective of what's happening when you actually move to the execution phase.

This might not really be required for the other phases. It's only meant for the implementation phase, because after the planning phase you are left with a DAG. DAG manages the dependencies, and you also have a bunch of features which are organized in DAGs. As the implementation phase proceeds, you have all these features getting implemented and tested and getting deployed and validated. We need to really think through that experience, and I wanted to put together a proposal, a plan of how to make this happen, so let's think through this very carefully.

I don't want to kill the current experience. The current experience is great. This is likely to be some kind of a supplemental experience, only for the implementation phase where you are able to double-click into how things are flying from, as and when the dependencies are getting satisfied. They are flowing through the implementation phase, getting tested, and basically deployed. Seeing that in action is going to be extremely, extremely kind of impactful, and I wanted to ensure that that experience is kind of thought through effectively.

**Key Features:**
- `useElapsedTime` — reactive composable that ticks every second via `setInterval`, cleans up via `onScopeDispose`, and formats elapsed seconds into `Xs`, `Xm Xs`, or `Xh Xm` strings
- `FlowFeatureCard` — compact feature card with stage-colored left border, pulsing agent dot when `building`, dep-fill progress bar, wave badge, priority badge, and elapsed time display
- `FlowProgressHeader` — sticky header with completion progress bar, feature count, current wave, and in-progress count; shows "ALL COMPLETE" badge when `dagComplete` is true
- `ImplementationFlow` — six-column Kanban layout driven by `STAGE_COLUMNS` from the store, with `TransitionGroup` animations for card movement between stages, auto-initializing from the DAG when `activeProjectId` changes

### Specification Compliance

- All four files match the specification exactly, including template structure, script logic, and scoped CSS
- Import paths match the existing project layout (`../stores/implementFlowStore`, `../composables/useElapsedTime`, etc.)

- `ImplementationFlow.vue` consumes `useImplementFlowStore` and `useWorkspaceStore` — both are Pinia stores already in the project

- `FlowProgressHeader.vue` is a pure presentational component — it only accepts `FlowProgress` and `dagComplete` as props with no store coupling
- `ImplementationFlow.vue` should be registered wherever the Implement phase panel is rendered (e.g., a router view or a tab panel in the main layout)</result>
